steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The list is 15, 14, 18, 17, $x$. The sum of the known values is $15 + 14 + 18 + 17 = 64$, so mean $= \frac{64 + x}{5}$. The median is the 3rd value when all five numbers are in order. Test each choice to see which makes mean $\neq$ median.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content: Test choice A, 6: if $x = 6$, the mean is $\frac{64+6}{5}=14$. Ordered, the list is $6,14,15,17,18$, so the median is $15$. Since the mean and median are not equal, $x = 6$ is not possible.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content:
• A: mean $14$, median $15$ — not equal, so this is the value that is not possible.
  • B: mean $15$, median $15$ — possible.
  • C: mean $16$, median $16$ — possible.
  • D: mean $17$, median $17$ — possible.
That is the correct answer.
